name: Restore accepted base, then test mirrored hot-band column sweep

summary.name: Restore accepted base, then test mirrored hot-band column sweep
summary.hypothesis: The 25.995744 ms regression came from the row-pair-dependent split column sweep. Restore the accepted grouped_rows=8 + reversed row-pair traversal + one-sync handoff baseline, then try a cleaner PTX hot-band variant that uses the mirrored column sweep uniformly for both row-pairs instead of branching the sweep by row-pair.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Bad instruction-flow and locality interaction from the failed row-pair-dependent column split, rather than the accepted base traversal itself.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: hot-band column-order / PTX sweep logic
summary.risk: Moderate: the mirrored sweep may recover performance only if the accepted base is fully restored first; keep the row-pair-dependent split branch closed.
summary.stop_condition: If the mirrored sweep does not beat the measured regression while preserving the accepted base behavior, close this branch and do not reopen the row-pair-dependent split.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
